root_text.an7.7:1.1: Atha kho uggo rājamahāmatto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho uggo rājamahāmatto bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an7.7:2.2: Yāva aḍḍho cāyaṁ, bhante, migāro rohaṇeyyo yāva mahaddhano yāva mahābhogo”ti.
root_text.an7.7:2.3: “Kīva aḍḍho panugga, migāro rohaṇeyyo, kīva mahaddhano, kīva mahābhogo”ti?
root_text.an7.7:2.4: “Sataṁ, bhante, satasahassānaṁ hiraññassa, ko pana vādo rūpiyassā”ti.
root_text.an7.7:2.5: “Atthi kho etaṁ, ugga, dhanaṁ netaṁ ‘natthī’ti vadāmīti.
root_text.an7.7:2.6: Tañca kho etaṁ, ugga, dhanaṁ sādhāraṇaṁ agginā udakena rājūhi corehi appiyehi dāyādehi.
root_text.an7.7:2.7: Satta kho imāni, ugga, dhanāni asādhāraṇāni agginā udakena rājūhi corehi appiyehi dāyādehi.
root_text.an7.7:2.9: Saddhādhanaṁ, sīladhanaṁ, hirīdhanaṁ, ottappadhanaṁ, sutadhanaṁ, cāgadhanaṁ, paññādhanaṁ.
root_text.an7.7:2.10: Imāni kho, ugga, satta dhanāni asādhāraṇāni agginā udakena rājūhi corehi appiyehi dāyādehīti.

translation_text.an7.7:1.1: Then Ugga the government chief minister went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.an7.7:2.2: Migāra of Rohaṇa is so rich, so very wealthy.”
translation_text.an7.7:2.4: “He has a hundred thousand gold coins, not to mention the silver coins!”
translation_text.an7.7:2.5: “Well, Ugga, that is wealth, I can’t deny it.
translation_text.an7.7:2.6: But fire, water, rulers, thieves, and unloved heirs all take a share of that wealth.
translation_text.an7.7:2.7: There are these seven kinds of wealth that they can’t take a share of.
translation_text.an7.7:2.9: The wealth of faith, ethics, conscience, prudence, learning, generosity, and wisdom.
translation_text.an7.7:2.10: There are these seven kinds of wealth that fire, water, rulers, thieves, and unloved heirs can’t take a share of.
translation_text.an7.7:3.1: Faith and ethical conduct are kinds of wealth,
translation_text.an7.7:3.4: and wisdom is the seventh kind of wealth.
translation_text.an7.7:4.3: they’re really rich in the world,
translation_text.an7.7:5.1: So let the wise devote themselves
translation_text.an7.7:5.3: confidence, and insight into the teaching,
translation_text.an7.7:5.4: remembering the instructions of the Buddhas.”
